req.title: Inter-facility referral (audit)
req.statement: A clinician shall send a referral to another facility; the referral shall trigger a secure message and consent check. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates three verifiable behaviours: (1) a valid referral from a consented clinician is stored and triggers a secure message plus an AuditEvent, (2) a referral where consent is absent is rejected with HTTP 403 and an AuditEvent is still emitted for the failed attempt, and (3) a boundary scenario where the referral payload sits at the minimum acceptable size (exactly one required field populated, all optional fields absent) still succeeds end-to-end. EP covers the happy-path partition of valid clinician + consent. NEG covers the absent-consent partition. BVA covers the minimum-viable payload boundary. STC and DT are embedded implicitly in the state transitions tested across the three cases.

tests.name: test_referral_valid_consent_stores_and_audits
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
Patient consent record exists for target facility
fhir-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: POST /ReferralRequest with valid payload and Authorization header
expected: Response body contains resourceType=ReferralRequest and an id
At least one AuditEvent recorded for the operation
SecureMessage record exists and is marked dispatched
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_referral_valid_consent_stores_and_audits(clinician_auth_token, base_url, consent_patient_id):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_auth_token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ReferralRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{consent_patient_id}"},
        "recipient": [{"reference": "Organization/facility-b"}]
    }

    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/ReferralRequest", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"

    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "ReferralRequest"
    referral_id = body["id"]
    assert referral_id

    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": referral_id},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    audit_entries = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "No AuditEvent found for referral"

    msg_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/SecureMessage",
        params={"referral": referral_id},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert msg_resp.status_code == 200
    messages = msg_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert any(m["resource"].get("status") == "dispatched" for m in messages), "No dispatched SecureMessage"

tests.name: test_referral_absent_consent_returns_403_and_audits
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
Patient record exists but consent has been explicitly revoked or never granted
fhir-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
actions: POST /ReferralRequest with valid structure but patient lacking consent
GET /AuditEvent?entity=<patient_id>&outcome=denied to verify denial is audited
expected: Response body contains OperationOutcome with consent-related diagnostics
AuditEvent with outcome=denied is emitted even for the rejected attempt
trace: R-HLT-092: AuditEvent emitted for every operation including failures
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_referral_absent_consent_returns_403_and_audits(clinician_auth_token, base_url, no_consent_patient_id):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_auth_token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ReferralRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{no_consent_patient_id}"},
        "recipient": [{"reference": "Organization/facility-b"}]
    }

    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/ReferralRequest", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"

    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome", "Expected OperationOutcome on 403"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert any("consent" in str(i).lower() for i in issues), "Expected consent-related issue in OperationOutcome"

    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": no_consent_patient_id, "outcome": "denied"},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    audit_entries = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "Denied attempt must still produce an AuditEvent"

tests.name: test_referral_minimum_viable_payload_boundary
preconditions: Authenticated clinician token available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
Patient consent record exists for target facility
fhir-lite service is reachable at BASE_URL
Payload contains ONLY the single mandatory field (subject) with all optional fields omitted
actions: POST /ReferralRequest with bare-minimum payload: resourceType + status + subject only
expected: POST /ReferralRequest returns HTTP 201 (system accepts minimum valid payload)
Response body contains a valid referral id
AuditEvent is emitted despite sparse payload
trace: BVA: lower boundary of payload completeness — exactly required fields, zero optional fields
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_referral_minimum_viable_payload_boundary(clinician_auth_token, base_url, consent_patient_id):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_auth_token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    # BVA: only mandatory fields; no recipient, no priority, no serviceRequested
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "ReferralRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{consent_patient_id}"}
    }

    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/ReferralRequest", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, (
        f"Minimum-viable payload should be accepted (201), got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )

    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "ReferralRequest"
    referral_id = body.get("id")
    assert referral_id, "Response must contain a referral id"

    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": referral_id},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    audit_entries = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "AuditEvent must be emitted even for minimum-payload referral"
